prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: most electrophilic condensed Fukui function f⁺ on formaldehyde (headline value is the largest f⁺ component)
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted) in PySCF
Geometry provenance: supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz; single-point N, N + 1, and N – 1 calculations – no further optimisation (DFT//given xyz)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) reference; cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) states for f ± evaluation
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF backend; version as bundled with chemkit)
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles converged for all three charge states. Engine reported three warnings (verbatim below). Sum-rule checks passed (Σf⁺ = Σf⁻ = 1.0000). No other warnings.
Live .out log: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/argo_o3/20260703-193720_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01.out (written live during the run; still available for inspection).

Results (Mulliken partitioning)
Atom-wise f⁺  = [0.46814 (O), 0.70839 (C), –0.08827 (H), –0.08826 (H)]
Atom-wise f⁻  = [0.46742 (O), 0.11244 (C),  0.21008 (H),  0.21006 (H)]
Dual descriptor Δf = f⁺ – f⁻ = [0.00071, 0.59595, –0.29835, –0.29832]

Most electrophilic site (max f⁺): atom 2 (C) with f⁺ = 0.7083913
Most nucleophilic site (max f⁻): atom 1 (O) with f⁻ = 0.46742464
